Methodology.sectionSubtitle: From the first site visit to secure report delivery — every step is governed by international standards and executed by certified engineers.
Methodology.box1Desc: ISO-9001 compliant micro-drilling at GPR-guided points. Core samples tested for compressive strength, carbonation depth, and chloride penetration in an accredited laboratory.
Methodology.box2Desc: EN 1998 Eurocode 8 seismic simulation of regional geological events. Every load-bearing frame is stress-tested against Turkey's TBDY 2018 seismic zone parameters for a full safety rating.
Methodology.box3Desc: Your report is cryptographically signed and stored. No third party can alter or duplicate your data. Access is secured via a unique PIN issued exclusively to you.

Value.p1: Buying, selling, insuring, or managing a property without a certified structural assessment means making decisions based on assumption. Our reports replace guesswork with documented, lab-verified facts.
Value.p2: From earthquake resilience to hidden concrete degradation, we assess every dimension of structural health — then deliver it in a tamper-proof, legally recognized format that protects your investment for years to come.
Value.feat1Desc: Laboratory-tested core samples confirm actual compressive strength against EN 12390 standards — not estimates, not visual inspections. Real numbers from real material.
Value.feat2Desc: Your building's seismic vulnerability rated under Eurocode 8 and TBDY 2018, with a simulated performance profile for Magnitude 7.0–7.5 events in your specific seismic zone.
Value.feat3Desc: A certified AURA report gives buyers, brokers, and notaries documented evidence of structural soundness — a concrete competitive advantage in any property transaction.
Value.feat4Desc: Carbonation mapping, chloride ion testing, and rebar corrosion assessment reveal deterioration that isn't visible to the eye — before it escalates into major structural failure.
Value.feat5Desc: Many Turkish banks and international insurers now require structural certification for high-value properties. Our reports meet all regulatory requirements out of the box.
Value.feat6Desc: Optional thermal imaging scan identifies heat loss, moisture ingress, and insulation failure — supporting energy upgrade planning and reducing operational costs.

Pricing.sectionSubtitle: A single, transparent package. No hidden fees. Full Eurocode 8 certification delivered in 14 days.
Pricing.cardDesc: Everything you need to certify the structural integrity of any residential or commercial property in the Aegean region.
Pricing.step1Desc: Our certified engineers arrive on-site, conduct a GPR subsurface scan, and perform precision micro-drilling at radar-identified critical points. Minimal disruption, maximum precision.
Pricing.step2Desc: Core samples undergo compressive strength, carbonation, and chloride testing in an ISO/IEC 17025 accredited lab. In parallel, we run a full Eurocode 8 seismic hazard simulation for your building.
Pricing.step3Desc: Your certified report is compiled, cryptographically signed, and made available via a secure personal PIN. A senior engineer walks you through the findings and recommendations.

FAQ.sectionSubtitle: Everything you need to know about our process, reports, and privacy.
FAQ.q1: Does the drilling damage my building?
FAQ.a1: No. We use diamond-tipped micro-drill bits (maximum Ø50mm diameter) at points selected via GPR scan to avoid reinforcement bars. Each hole is immediately filled with high-strength structural epoxy (minimum 40 MPa) — fully restoring the element. The entire extraction process is ISO-9001 compliant and leaves no lasting trace.
FAQ.q2: How do I access my report once it's ready?
FAQ.a2: When your report is finalised, you receive a unique 4–6 digit PIN. Simply visit this portal, search for your building by name or ID, enter your PIN, and your full certified PDF report is instantly available to view and download — from anywhere, at any time.
FAQ.q3: Is this report accepted by Turkish banks and authorities?
FAQ.a3: Yes. Our reports comply with TS 500, TBDY 2018, and Eurocode 8 standards, and are recognised by Turkish municipal authorities for permit applications, by banks for mortgage assessments, and by insurance companies for coverage decisions on high-value properties.
FAQ.q4: What happens if serious problems are found?
FAQ.a4: If our analysis identifies a structural concern — such as rebar corrosion, seismic under-performance, or concrete degradation — we notify you privately before the report is finalised. A senior engineer consults with you one-on-one to explain the findings, their implications, and the available remediation options.

ContactPage.title: Connect With Our
ContactPage.subtitle: Engage our structural advisory team to discuss your asset portfolio with complete institutional discretion. All communications are AES-256 encrypted and handled under strict KVKK/GDPR compliance.
ContactPage.formSubtitle: Our advisory team responds within 24 business hours.

ContactPage.privacyNote: AES-256 Encrypted · KVKK / GDPR Compliant · Zero-Knowledge Protocol

ContactPage.officesSubtitle: Our dedicated engineering nodes are strategically positioned across the Aegean sector for rapid on-site deployment.
ContactPage.node1Desc: Primary operations centre for the Western Aegean sector. Full GPR and laboratory capabilities on-site.
ContactPage.node2Desc: Secondary hub serving the Bodrum Peninsula and surrounding municipalities. Seismic monitoring station.
ContactPage.node3Desc: Geological monitoring and rapid-response unit for the Eastern Mediterranean corridor.

AboutPage.title: Engineering
AboutPage.subtitle: AURA Analytics was founded with a singular mission: to bring institutional-grade structural intelligence to the modern real estate market through science, cryptography, and engineering rigor.
AboutPage.missionDesc: To provide cryptographically verified, Eurocode-compliant structural analysis that empowers property owners, investors, and institutions to make decisions with absolute confidence in structural data.
AboutPage.visionDesc: A world where every significant property transaction is backed by immutable, scientifically verified structural intelligence — not assumptions or incomplete assessments.
AboutPage.valuesSubtitle: The principles that guide every analysis, every report, and every client interaction.
AboutPage.val1Desc: Every analysis adheres strictly to ISO-9001 protocols and Eurocode 8 standards, ensuring the highest level of scientific accuracy and full reproducibility of results.
AboutPage.val2Desc: We deploy zero-knowledge proofs and AES-256 encryption. Your property data remains yours alone, accessible only via your unique cryptographic credential.
AboutPage.val3Desc: Our reports are accepted by leading real estate brokerages, insurance firms, and private wealth institutions across Europe and the Middle East.
AboutPage.val4Desc: Continuously advancing our algorithms and methodologies to remain at the forefront of geological data science, Eurocode updates, and material science.
AboutPage.val5Desc: We provide strategic guidance throughout the entire process — from initial scoping to post-audit consultation and remediation roadmaps.
AboutPage.val6Desc: Our assessments include energy efficiency insights, helping property owners understand their environmental footprint and identify meaningful reduction opportunities.
AboutPage.teamSubtitle: A multidisciplinary team of registered structural engineers, data scientists, and certified seismic analysts.
AboutPage.m1Desc: 20+ years of structural analysis experience across Turkey, Germany, and the UK. ISO-9001 lead auditor and TMMOB registered engineer.
AboutPage.m2Desc: Specialist in geological event simulation and high-magnitude seismic stress modeling for complex mixed-use architectural structures.
AboutPage.m3Desc: Architect of our immutable distributed ledger system, ensuring full KVKK and GDPR compliance across all edge nodes.
AboutPage.m4Desc: Leading expert in concrete micro-structure analysis and non-destructive testing using ultrasonic resonance and carbonation mapping technology.
AboutPage.certSubtitle: Our work is governed, accredited, and independently verified by internationally recognized bodies.
AboutPage.c4: TMMOB – Turkish Chamber of Civil Engineers

KVKKPage.title: KVKK / GDPR
KVKKPage.subtitle: How we collect, process, and protect your personal data under Turkish Law No. 6698 (KVKK) and EU Regulation 2016/679 (GDPR).
KVKKPage.controllerDesc: Aura Engineering Ltd. ('AURA', 'we', 'us') acts as the data controller for all personal data collected through this platform. Registered address: Kalkan, Antalya, Turkey. Contact: privacy@aura.systems.
KVKKPage.s1Content: We collect the following categories of personal data: (a) Contact information provided voluntarily through our contact and engagement forms — including full name, email address, and phone number; (b) Property information provided for structural analysis purposes — including property address, type, and physical parameters; (c) Technical data — including IP address, browser type, device identifiers, and interaction logs; (d) Analysis-related data — including building photographs and structural assessment documentation.
KVKKPage.s2Content: Under KVKK Article 5 and GDPR Article 6, we process your data on the following legal bases: (a) Contractual necessity — processing required to deliver the structural analysis services you have engaged; (b) Legitimate interests — operating our platform securely and improving our services; (c) Legal obligation — complying with applicable Turkish and European regulations; (d) Consent — where you have explicitly opted in to receive communications.
KVKKPage.s3Content: Your personal data is processed for: delivering structural analysis reports and related engineering services; responding to contact and enquiry submissions; processing engagement and quotation requests; maintaining the security and integrity of the platform; complying with applicable legal and regulatory obligations; and improving our analysis methodologies and service delivery.
KVKKPage.s4Content: As a data subject, you hold the following rights: the right to be informed about the processing of your data; the right to access your personal data; the right to rectification of inaccurate data; the right to erasure ('right to be forgotten'); the right to restriction of processing; the right to data portability; the right to object to processing; and the right not to be subject to automated decision-making. To exercise any of these rights, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@aura.systems.
KVKKPage.s5Content: Personal data is retained only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected. Analysis reports and associated data are retained for a minimum of 10 years as required by Turkish engineering and construction regulations. Contact and enquiry data is deleted within 2 years of last interaction. Technical log data is purged after 12 months.
KVKKPage.s6Content: All personal data is protected using AES-256 encryption at rest and in transit. Access to personal data is restricted to authorised personnel only. We implement zero-knowledge cryptographic proof systems to ensure that report data is accessible only by the legitimate property owner. Regular penetration testing and security audits are conducted in accordance with ISO/IEC 27001 principles.
KVKKPage.s7Content: We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data. Data may be shared with: accredited laboratory partners for the sole purpose of conducting material testing; regulatory authorities where required by law; and our infrastructure providers under strict data processing agreements conforming to KVKK and GDPR requirements. All third-party processors are contractually bound to equivalent data protection standards.
KVKKPage.s8Content: This platform uses strictly necessary cookies to maintain session security and platform functionality. We do not use third-party advertising cookies or cross-site tracking technologies. Technical cookies are automatically deleted when you close your browser session. No personal data is collected via cookies beyond what is required for the secure operation of the platform.
KVKKPage.s9Content: We may update this policy periodically to reflect changes in our practices or applicable legal requirements. The effective date of the latest version is displayed above. Continued use of the platform following any update constitutes acceptance of the revised policy. For material changes, we will provide a prominent notice on the platform.
KVKKPage.rightsTitle: Your Data Rights at a Glance
KVKKPage.rightsSubtitle: A summary of your rights under KVKK Article 11 and GDPR Articles 15–22.
KVKKPage.contactDesc: For all data protection enquiries, to exercise your rights, or to file a complaint, contact our Data Protection Officer. We respond to all requests within 30 days in accordance with KVKK and GDPR requirements.
KVKKPage.dpoNote: Response guaranteed within 30 calendar days · KVKK Art. 13 / GDPR Art. 12

PrivacyPage.title: Privacy
PrivacyPage.subtitle: This Privacy Policy explains how Aura Engineering collects, uses, stores, and protects your information when you use our platform and structural analysis services.
PrivacyPage.pillEncryption: AES-256 Encrypted at Rest & in Transit
PrivacyPage.s1Content: We collect information you provide directly — such as your name, email, phone number, and property details — when you submit forms, request a quote, or use our building search feature. We also collect technical data automatically, including your IP address, browser type, and usage patterns, to ensure platform security and performance.
PrivacyPage.s2Content: Information you provide is used to: process and deliver structural analysis services; respond to your enquiries and support requests; send service-related communications; improve the quality of our analysis methodology and platform; and comply with our legal and regulatory obligations under Turkish and EU law.
PrivacyPage.s3Content: Access to structural analysis reports is protected by a unique PIN issued exclusively to you at service completion. We do not store PINs in plaintext — they are cryptographically hashed using industry-standard algorithms. We will never ask for your PIN via email, phone, or any channel other than the secure portal input. If you suspect your PIN has been compromised, contact us immediately.
PrivacyPage.s4Content: We use session cookies that are strictly necessary for platform security and functionality — for example, to maintain your session state while navigating the report portal. We do not use tracking pixels, advertising cookies, or third-party analytics scripts. You can disable cookies in your browser settings, but this may impair platform functionality.
PrivacyPage.s5Content: We do not sell your personal information. We share data only with trusted partners who assist in delivering our services — including ISO/IEC 17025 accredited laboratories for material testing — under strict contractual data protection obligations. We may disclose data to competent authorities where legally required to do so.
PrivacyPage.s6Content: We employ industry-leading security measures including AES-256 encryption for all data at rest and in transit, strict access controls, regular security audits, and zero-knowledge cryptographic proof systems for report delivery. Our infrastructure is hosted in certified data centres with physical and logical access controls.
PrivacyPage.s7Content: We retain your personal data only as long as necessary. Analysis reports are retained for 10 years in compliance with Turkish construction law. Enquiry and contact data is deleted after 2 years. You may request deletion of your data at any time by contacting privacy@aura.systems, subject to our legal retention obligations.
PrivacyPage.s8Content: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data; to restrict or object to its processing; and to receive a copy in a portable format. To exercise these rights or to make a complaint, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@aura.systems. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Turkish Personal Data Protection Authority (KVKK Kurumu) or your local EU supervisory authority.
PrivacyPage.s9Content: Our primary operations and data storage are located within Turkey. Where data is transferred to countries outside Turkey or the EU/EEA, we ensure adequate safeguards are in place in accordance with KVKK and GDPR transfer mechanisms, including standard contractual clauses approved by the relevant supervisory authorities.
PrivacyPage.s10Content: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we do, we will revise the 'Last Updated' date at the top of this page. We encourage you to review this policy periodically. Material changes will be communicated through a prominent notice on our platform.
PrivacyPage.contactDesc: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please contact our Data Protection Officer directly.

ProceduresPage.title: Analysis Procedures &
ProceduresPage.subtitle: Our comprehensive building analysis methodology is governed by international standards and regulatory frameworks, ensuring absolute structural certainty and full legal compliance.
ProceduresPage.processSubtitle: A rigorous, phased approach to structural assessment executed by certified engineers under ISO-9001 quality management protocols — from first site visit to cryptographic report delivery.
ProceduresPage.ph1Desc: Initial desk study and non-invasive visual inspection to identify structural typology, materials, building age, and potential vulnerability zones prior to physical intervention.
ProceduresPage.ph2Desc: Ground-penetrating radar (GPR) scanning of all load-bearing walls, floors, and foundations to generate a precise 3D subsurface model used to guide core sampling.
ProceduresPage.ph3Desc: ISO-9001 compliant surgical micro-drilling at radar-identified critical points. Diamond-tipped drill bits (Ø50mm max). All extraction holes immediately sealed with high-strength structural epoxy (≥40 MPa).
ProceduresPage.ph4Desc: Comprehensive testing of core samples including compressive strength (TS EN 12390-3), carbonation depth, chloride ion penetration, and reinforcement corrosion potential assessment in ISO/IEC 17025 accredited facilities.
ProceduresPage.ph5Desc: High-compute Eurocode 8 (EN 1998) seismic hazard analysis simulating regional geological events. Dynamic stress-testing of the complete structural frame against TBDY 2018 seismic zone parameters.
ProceduresPage.ph6Desc: Compilation of all findings into a cryptographically hashed, immutable ledger entry. Report secured with AES-256 encryption. Unique access PIN hand-delivered directly to the client.
ProceduresPage.licSubtitle: Every analysis is underpinned by internationally recognized standards and held to the highest level of regulatory compliance.
ProceduresPage.lic1Desc: Our entire operational workflow — from site survey to cryptographic delivery — adheres to ISO 9001:2015 quality management protocols, ensuring consistency, full traceability, and continuous improvement at every stage.
ProceduresPage.lic2Desc: All seismic analyses conform to EN 1998 (Eurocode 8), the European standard for earthquake-resistant structural design. Our simulation algorithms are calibrated against regional seismic hazard maps and TBDY 2018.
ProceduresPage.lic3Desc: Core sample laboratory testing is performed exclusively in ISO/IEC 17025:2017 accredited facilities, guaranteeing measurement accuracy, validated test methods, and fully impartial reporting.
ProceduresPage.lic4Desc: All personal and property data is processed under strict KVKK (Turkish Law No. 6698) and GDPR (EU Regulation 2016/679) compliance frameworks. Zero-knowledge cryptographic proofs ensure absolute data privacy.
ProceduresPage.lic5Desc: Structural certification reports carry CE marking in accordance with Construction Products Regulation (EU) No 305/2011, enabling their recognized use across all EU member states and associated markets.
ProceduresPage.lic6Desc: All on-site engineering activities are performed exclusively by registered members of TMMOB — the national regulatory body under the Union of Chambers of Turkish Engineers and Architects.
ProceduresPage.regSubtitle: Our analyses comply with all applicable Turkish and European structural engineering regulations and standards.
ProceduresPage.reg1: TS 500 – Requirements for Design and Construction of Reinforced Concrete Structures
ProceduresPage.reg4: EN 1998 (Eurocode 8) – Earthquake Resistant Design of Structures
ProceduresPage.reg5: TS EN 12390 – Testing Hardened Concrete (Compressive Strength)
ProceduresPage.reg6: FEMA P-58 – Seismic Performance Assessment of Buildings
ProceduresPage.faq1Q: How long does a complete analysis take from start to finish?
ProceduresPage.faq1A: A full building analysis — from initial site survey to cryptographic report delivery — takes approximately 14 calendar days. This comprises 3 days of on-site work (survey, GPR, and core extraction), 5–7 days of accredited laboratory analysis, and 3–4 days for seismic simulation and final report generation.
ProceduresPage.faq2Q: Does micro-drilling cause any structural damage?
ProceduresPage.faq2A: No. Our surgical micro-drilling strictly follows ISO-9001 protocols using diamond-tipped drill bits (maximum Ø50mm diameter) at points strategically selected via GPR scanning to avoid critical reinforcement. All extraction holes are immediately sealed with high-strength structural epoxy (minimum 40 MPa compressive strength), fully restoring the structural integrity of the element.
ProceduresPage.faq3Q: What professional certifications do your engineers hold?
ProceduresPage.faq3A: All field engineers are registered members of TMMOB (Turkish Chamber of Civil Engineers) and hold valid professional engineering licenses. Seismic analysts are certified to Eurocode 8 standards. All laboratory testing is conducted in ISO/IEC 17025:2017 accredited facilities by qualified technicians.
ProceduresPage.faq4Q: Are your reports legally recognized by Turkish authorities?
ProceduresPage.faq4A: Yes. Our reports comply fully with TS 500, TBDY 2018, and Eurocode 8 standards and are recognized by Turkish municipal authorities for building permit applications, insurance assessments, and property transactions. The cryptographic hash provides an additional layer of immutable, tamper-proof verification.
